conventions.name: app-desktop-must-not-import-contracts
conventions.description: Post-cut boundary: app and desktop must use shared/core surfaces instead of the deleted contracts package.

conventions.name: app-desktop-must-not-import-runtime
conventions.description: Post-cut boundary: app and desktop must not import the deleted runtime package.

conventions.name: app-desktop-must-not-import-client-runtime
conventions.description: Post-cut boundary: app and desktop must not import the deleted client-runtime package.

conventions.name: public-source-indexes-are-barrels
conventions.description: Public package indexes stay declarative; implementation belongs in named modules.

conventions.name: platform-components-have-shared-contracts
conventions.description: A native UI renderer is added only as a complete web/native pair over one product-level contract.

conventions.name: opencode-package-entrypoints
conventions.description: @honk/opencode keeps one explicit client boundary with separate host and native adapters.

conventions.name: opencode-host-plugin-shape
conventions.description: The generated OpenCode host plugin keeps behavior, shared types, agents, pairing, and tool hooks in named modules.

conventions.name: opencode-native-entrypoint-contract
conventions.description: The native adapter exposes the same event-stream factory contract consumed by the shared client.

conventions.name: opencode-client-contract
conventions.description: The OpenCode client keeps a small PI-like explicit surface instead of leaking SDK implementation details.

conventions.name: opencode-event-source-contract
conventions.description: Web and native event transports implement one renderer-neutral event-source contract.

conventions.name: opencode-connection-contract
conventions.description: Connection parsing and pairing remain explicit named APIs at the OpenCode boundary.

conventions.name: opencode-host-entrypoint-contract
conventions.description: The host entrypoint keeps its config builder and managed plugin constants explicit.

conventions.name: opencode-host-plugin-entrypoint-contract
conventions.description: The generated host plugin preserves OpenCode's explicit id, setup, and server hooks.

conventions.name: opencode-boundary-must-not-import-effect-barrel
conventions.description: @honk/opencode remains a transport boundary instead of becoming a second Effect runtime.

conventions.name: opencode-boundary-must-not-import-effect-modules
conventions.description: @honk/opencode remains a transport boundary instead of becoming a second Effect runtime.

conventions.name: opencode-boundary-must-not-import-pi
conventions.description: PI is an implementation reference, not a second client protocol inside @honk/opencode.

conventions.name: shared-contracts-must-not-import-opencode-sdk
conventions.description: Representation-neutral shared contracts do not leak the upstream OpenCode SDK.

conventions.name: shared-contracts-must-not-import-pi
conventions.description: Representation-neutral shared contracts do not depend on PI implementation packages.
